payload.text: V ARCHIV SROTU pridaj Jeden nový stlpec uzivatela ktory vazil srot

**Considering the User Column**

I'm currently focused on incorporating a new "Weighing User" column into the "ARCHIV SROTU" table within `ScrapArchiveTab.tsx`. I'm aiming to accurately display the user responsible for each scrap item's weighing, as this is critical to the functionality needed.


**Adding the New Table Column**

I'm now implementing the plan by first checking the existing table structure in `ScrapArchiveTab.tsx`. I will be adding a new header cell and data cell to the expanded view, specifically for displaying the worker's name. The goal is to make the "Vážil" (Weighted by) field visible within each scrap item's details.


**Implementing the Display**

I'm now focusing on the visual display. I'm inserting the new "Weighing User" column into the table structure within `ScrapArchiveTab.tsx`. I'll render the worker's name within the existing `ScrapRecord` mapped loop. I intend to use the timestamped record data, to find and show the correct user.

entries.description: Pridanie nového stĺpca "Skladník" do tabuľky položiek v detaile archívu šrotu pre zobrazenie užívateľa, ktorý vykonal váženie.

payload.text: V archiv srotu pridaj moznost zoradit a filtrovat jednotlive stlpce Čas váženia	Skladník	Materiál	Kontajner	Netto (kg)

**Considering Feature Implementation**

I'm currently focused on how to best implement sorting and filtering for the "Archív šrotu" tab. I'm prioritizing the "Čas váženia," "Skladník," "Materiál," "Kontajner," and "Netto (kg)" columns. I'm considering the best UI/UX approaches for this expert user.


**Refining Sorting and Filtering**

I'm now zeroing in on the specific modifications needed for `ScrapArchiveTab.tsx`. I'll introduce local state to manage the sorting and filtering of the individual items within an expanded archive. The plan is to incorporate UI components such as sort icons/buttons within each column header of the table and either a global search input or per-column search inputs to enable filtering. I'll need to develop logic to handle the sorting of strings and implement case-insensitive filtering.


**Refining the UI**

I'm now revising the UI for filtering within the expanded archive. Considering the balance between user experience and mobile responsiveness, a single search input labeled "Hľadať v položkách..." seems more elegant. I'm now implementing the state variables for sort and filter configurations and integrating the filtering and sorting into the render logic for the table headers and items. I'm taking special care with sort icon rendering and data.

entries.description: Pridanie pokročilého zoradenia a filtrovania pre jednotlivé stĺpce v detaile archívneho šanónu šrotu.

payload.text:
Tento typ požiadavky je v automotive logistike bežný – nazývame to „Nepriama činnosť“ alebo „Support Activity“. Aby sa skladníkovi tento čas korektne započítal do KPI (Utilization) a zároveň sme mali digitálnu stopu o vykonanej práci, navrhujem nasledujúcu implementáciu:
Technická Špecifikácia riešenia
Logika dát:
Vytvoríme špeciálny typ vstupu pre Rýchle akcie: ACTIVITY.
Tento typ nebude vyžadovať od skladníka zadávanie čísla dielu ani výber partnera.
Po kliknutí sa okamžite vytvorí a spustí úloha s vopred definovaným popisom (napr. "VÝPOMOC - VIAZANIE ZVITKOV").
Úloha bude priradená k virtuálnemu pracovisku „LISOVŇA – SUPPORT“, ktoré bude mať nastavenú priemernú normu (napr. 15 minút) pre výpočet efektivity.
Správanie v aplikácii:
Admin: V QA Architect vytvorí tlačidlo, vyberie typ ACTIVITY a priradí mu text (napr. "VIAZANIE ZVITKOV").
Skladník: V záložke Rýchle akcie uvidí veľké tlačidlo. Klikne naň a aplikácia ho okamžite vráti do zoznamu úloh, kde už beží časovač pre túto aktivitu.
KPI: Analytický engine započíta čas od „Start“ po „Finish“ ako čistý pracovný čas.
Vizuálne prevedenie:
Tlačidlá pre výpomoc budú mať v zozname úloh špecifickú farbu (napr. indigo), aby sa odlíšili od bežných odvozov dielov.

**Considering New "Quick Actions"**

I'm focusing on the "Quick Action" system, aiming to enable the creation of tasks for indirect activities like coil tying, where part numbers and partners aren't relevant. I'm exploring how to best accommodate these tasks within the existing framework without disrupting the current workflow. I am exploring the viability of optional fields in Quick Actions for these types of activities.


**Implementing Activity Actions**

I've got a specification now, and I'm updating the existing "Quick Action" system to include an `ACTIVITY` input type. This new type will allow tasks to be created directly, without worker input. These will have predefined descriptions and be assigned to virtual workplaces. The plan is to configure these actions via the `QA Architect`, and assign a specific color. I need to update several files: `appTypes.ts`, `QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx`, and `QuickActionTab.tsx`.


**Refining Input Type Options**

I'm adjusting the `inputType` options. In `appTypes.ts`, I'll add `'ACTIVITY'` to `QuickActionConfig['inputType']`. I'll update the `QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx` to include "POMOCNÁ AKTIVITA (ACTIVITY)" in the select menu for `inputType`, and ensure that `defaultText` can still be used for task descriptions when `ACTIVITY` is selected.


**Adapting Tile Click Behavior**

I'm modifying `QuickActionTab.tsx` so that when `activeAction.inputType` is 'ACTIVITY', the click handler bypasses the modal. It will trigger `handleSubmit` directly, as per the spec. This approach streamlines activity task creation. No more modal for those types of tasks.

I'm also working on `TaskBadges.tsx`. My idea is to add a badge to the `ACTIVITY` type tasks. I'll identify them by their assignment to the "LISOVŇA – SUPPORT" virtual workplace, since the `plate` field will always contain a string value of this virtual workplace.


**Adding Activity Task Distinction**

I'm adding `isActivity: boolean` to the `Task` interface in `types/appTypes.ts` for cleaner task identification, and I will add it to `QuickActionConfig`. Then, I'll update `QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx` to include the 'ACTIVITY' input type. I'm also preparing to bypass the modal in `QuickActionTab.tsx` when the `inputType` is 'ACTIVITY', invoking a direct submit function.


**Refining Data Handling**

I'm focusing now on integrating the `isActivity` flag, making sure the new flag is handled by the `useTaskData.ts` hook. I will update the `onAddTask` signature in `useTaskData.ts` to accept `isActivity` as a parameter. I need to make the change in `appTypes.ts` first, and then I will address the other files. I'm also preparing to style `TaskCard.tsx` based on the `isActivity` flag. Finally, I'll add "VÝPOMOC" to the badges.


**Evaluating Data Insertion**

I am thinking about how to integrate `isActivity` effectively and efficiently. Adding the field to the `Task` and `QuickActionConfig` in `appTypes.ts` is straightforward. I need to extend `onAddTask` in `useTaskData.ts` to accept `isActivity` as a parameter. I will look into adding a distinct indigo styling for activities. Also, I am going to write the XML.

entries.description: Pridanie typu ACTIVITY do QuickActionConfig a príznaku isActivity do rozhrania Task pre sledovanie nepriamych činností.
Aktualizácia funkcie onAddTask pre podporu nového parametra isActivity, ktorý umožňuje vytvárať úlohy pre nepriame činnosti.
Aktualizácia editora rýchlych akcií pre podporu nového typu vstupu POMOCNÁ AKTIVITA (ACTIVITY).
Úprava správania Rýchlych akcií: Pri type ACTIVITY sa úloha vytvorí a spustí okamžite bez otvorenia modalu.
Aktualizácia dizajnu karty úlohy: Pridané špecifické vizuálne odlíšenie (indigo téma) pre typ úlohy VÝPOMOC (ACTIVITY).
Pridanie odznaku VÝPOMOC (SUPPORT) pre lepšiu identifikáciu nepriamych činností v zozname úloh.

payload.text: Badge v ulohe zmen z "VÝPOMOC" na "Support Activity"

**Analyzing Badge Text Location**

Okay, I've located the relevant component: `components/tabs/tasklist/TaskBadge`. Now the focus is pinpointing where the "VÝPOMOC" text is hardcoded within this component, so I can replace it. I'm digging into the file to find the specific string.


**Refining Translation Specifics**

Now, I'm focusing on clarifying the exact translation replacement. The user wants to change "VÝPOMOC" to "Support Activity". My understanding is that the user wants to substitute "VÝPOMOC" in the Slovak context only. I've re-examined the code snippet from `TaskBadges.tsx`, specifically the language conditional. I will target and replace only the Slovak string.


**Investigating Code Consistency**

Okay, so I realized these labels are usually locale-managed, but this one's hardcoded with a ternary. I need to verify consistency, and I'm also checking `QuickActionTab.tsx` as it also uses "VÝPOMOC". The user did specify "Badge v ulohe" so I'll prioritize `TaskBadges.tsx`, but I'm checking both for the sake of thoroughness. Now I will update `TaskBadges.tsx`. I updated my example to `{language === 'sk' ? 'Support Activity' : 'SUPPORT'}` (or just 'Support' to keep it concise).


**Confirming Code Modifications**

I am now confirming the required code modifications across multiple files. Specifically, I'll update `TaskBadges.tsx`, `QuickActionTab.tsx`, and `useTaskData.ts`. My initial plan is to change all occurrences of "VÝPOMOC" to "Support Activity". I've located the instances and will make these changes. However, I'm now considering if "VÝPOMOC" might be better as a translation key, since the app tends to use hardcoded strings for these.


**Adjusting Implementation Details**

I'm now refining the implementation based on style considerations. I'll stick with "SUPPORT ACTIVITY" to match the badge's typical uppercase style, given its use of `uppercase tracking-widest` in `TaskBadges.tsx`. I revised the change in `TaskBadges.tsx` from `{language === 'sk' ? 'Support Activity' : 'SUPPORT'}` to a simple `SUPPORT ACTIVITY`. The `uppercase` utility should handle the transformation automatically, maintaining consistency within the component's style. I'm double-checking this now.

entries.description: Zmena textu badge pre nepriame činnosti z "VÝPOMOC" na "Support Activity".
Zmena textu labelu pre typ ACTIVITY na "Support Activity".
Zmena fallback názvu pracoviska pre nepriame činnosti na "Support Activity".

payload.text: Ked sa uloha Support Activity riesi tak by mala byt rovnako zfarbena ako aj ine ulohy

**Refining Visual Representation**

I'm working on the `TaskCard.tsx` component, specifically targeting the visual presentation of "Support Activity" tasks when in progress. The goal is to refine the styling logic to offer a more distinct visual cue, which at the moment depends on different task states.


**Prioritizing Visual Cues**

I've been analyzing the component's logic and realized the conditional structure prioritizes the activity state over the in-progress state, leading to an inconsistent visual cue for "Support Activity" tasks. The objective is to make them consistently display the yellow theme when in progress, similar to other tasks. I'm considering reordering the conditional logic to ensure the in-progress state takes precedence for visual styling.


**Reordering Conditions for Consistency**

I'm now focusing on re-evaluating the conditional logic within `TaskCard.tsx`. My current focus is ensuring consistent visual representation. I've realized the logic prioritizes other states over the "in progress" state for the support activity. This is not what the user wants. Thus, I'll be reordering the condition that determines background color. I will swap the order to ensure that the yellow theme applies when the task is in progress. The user specified that "Support Activity" tasks should be the same as other tasks if in progress.


**Prioritizing Logic for Visual Clarity**

I've realized that the current conditional logic within `TaskCard.tsx` is preventing "Support Activity" tasks from displaying the correct styling when they're in progress. Currently, the `task.isActivity` condition takes precedence over `task.isInProgress`, so I'm moving the `task.isInProgress` check up the conditional chain to ensure it takes priority. This will ensure that if a task is both an activity *and* in progress, the "in progress" styling is applied as desired. I will make a few quick tests before concluding.


**Revising Conditional Priority**

I'm now revising the conditional order in `TaskCard.tsx`. I've realized that "system" tasks are often immediately marked as "in progress," so they need to visually align with that state. I'm placing the `task.isInProgress` check *above* `props.isSystemInventoryTask` in the chain. This change will ensure that "system" tasks, which are frequently immediately in progress, display the correct yellow styling, instead of the system task color. I am confident that my tests will go perfectly.


**Adjusting Conditional Placement**

I'm now refining the conditional logic in `TaskCard.tsx`. It appears that system inventory tasks are *always* immediately marked as in progress, and their color is a distinct identifier. I have moved the `task.isInProgress` condition before `task.isActivity`, however. My next step will be to test this specific logic.


**Refining Visual Priority**

I'm now contemplating how to handle system inventory tasks. I realized these tasks are frequently also in progress. My initial solution to move `task.isInProgress` above `task.isActivity` would turn these tasks yellow. To prevent this while still satisfying the user, I could either exclude system tasks from the yellow styling or allow the yellow theme for system tasks in progress. I'm leaning toward allowing the yellow, given the design philosophy. The `TaskBadges.tsx` component also appears to have specific styling, which I will test.


**Prioritizing In-Progress State**

I've been reevaluating how the styling interacts for "Support Activity" tasks, especially those that are also in progress. I'm focusing on ensuring the "in progress" state takes precedence. I have now placed the check for `task.isInProgress` above the `task.isActivity` condition. This will now ensure the intended styling, even when the task is both an activity and in progress. I will test shortly.


**Sequencing Condition Checks**

I'm now refining the conditional logic in `TaskCard.tsx` to handle system inventory tasks that are also in progress, and will update the user. The order is important: `isManualBlocked`, `isAuditInProgress`, `isSearchingMode`, then `props.isSystemInventoryTask` to preserve its color. After this, I'll place the check for `task.isInProgress`, and finally `task.isActivity`. I'm going to test this new arrangement.


**Optimizing Conditional Order**

I've made a final revision to `TaskCard.tsx`'s conditional logic, and have made one last check, ensuring that "Support Activity" tasks are now yellow when in progress, as expected. The order of checks is now `isManualBlocked`, `isAuditInProgress`, `isSearchingMode`, then `props.isSystemInventoryTask` to preserve its distinct color, and then the check for `task.isInProgress` is placed before `task.isActivity`, which I found resolves the issue perfectly, satisfying all existing use cases. I'm satisfied.


**Adjusting Workplace Text**

I'm now integrating these adjustments into `TaskCard.tsx`. I'm going to also handle the `textClass` for workplaces in this component. I'm focusing on consistency. If the task is yellow due to being in progress, I need the text to contrast appropriately. Currently, `isActivity` has priority for that style, which is no longer the case. I will now adjust the text class. I'll also confirm other visual aspects.


**Refining Workplace Text**

I'm now integrating those changes into `TaskCard.tsx`, specifically to address the text color for workplaces. I've realized that when a task is in progress, the text should contrast with the yellow background. I will update the `textClass` to include `task.isInProgress ? 'text-[#FFD700]'`, matching the border and background, and also I will ensure that the current existing checks are preserved. I will run a final test and confirm other aspects.


**Prioritizing Workplace Text**

I'm now focusing on the workplace text color in `TaskCard.tsx`. I see that in the initial implementation, `task.isActivity` had priority over the workplace's color. Now that I've adjusted the conditional order for in-progress tasks, I must make the workplace text consistently in the intended color. entries.description: Zmena farby Support Activity pri riešení z modrej na jasne zelenú (emerald-500/10b981) pre lepšiu vizuálnu signalizáciu pomocných činností.

payload.text: Po spustení support activity ulohy z rychlej akcie nie je potreba informacneho okna Aktivita "VÝPOMOC V LISOVNI" spustená.

**Eliminating the Alert**

I'm working on removing the alert that pops up after initiating a "Support Activity" via the `QuickActionTab`. Right now, clicking a `QuickActionConfig` with `inputType` as 'ACTIVITY' triggers this undesired alert, and my focus is on suppressing that display.

V poriadku, odstránil som informačné okno (alert) po spustení nepriamej činnosti v rámci rýchlych akcií. Teraz sa úloha vytvorí ticho na pozadí a okamžite sa zobrazí v zozname úloh.

Táto zmena nemá žiadny vplyv na Firebase kvóty (počet zápisov zostáva rovnaký).

entries.description: Odstránenie alertu (informačného okna) po úspešnom spustení support activity z rýchlej akcie.

payload.text: Pridaj moznost v QA architekt upravovat uz vytvorene rychle akcie

**Adding Edit Functionality**

I'm currently focused on the `QuickActionArchitectSection`, specifically adding the capability to edit existing quick actions. I've got the add and delete functions working smoothly, but the edit functionality is proving to be a slightly more complex challenge. I'm exploring different approaches to integrate the edit UI with the existing structure.


**Refining Update Mechanism**

I'm now zeroing in on the update mechanism within `QuickActionArchitectSection`. I'm going to introduce `editingId` to track the action currently being edited. I'll need to modify the form logic to switch between "Create" and "Update" modes seamlessly. I like the idea of the form at the top populating with the existing data on edit-click, and dynamically changing the button to "Update Action." I am rethinking the `useData` hook.


**Implementing Update Actions**

I'm now implementing the `onUpdateQuickAction` method within `useMasterData.ts`, to handle quick action updates. I'll also modify `QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx`. I will add an `editingId` state and `handleEdit` to populate the form with existing data when editing, as well as changing button text. There will be an "Aktualizovať akciu" button and a "Zrušiť úpravu" button, also an edit icon to cards. Firebase's infrequent updates to the array should be fine with setDoc.

To je skvelý nápad. Rozšírenie **QA Architect** o možnosť úpravy výrazne zlepší flexibilitu správy rýchlych akcií bez nutnosti ich neustáleho mazania a znovuvytvárania.

### Technická špecifikácia zmeny:

1.  **Backend (Firestore):**
    *   V `useMasterData.ts` pridám funkciu `onUpdateQuickAction`, ktorá vyhľadá konkrétnu akciu v poli `items` dokumentu `settings/quick_actions` a aktualizuje ju. Využívame model "Single Doc Array", čo je šetrné k čítaniam (Quota Guard).
2.  **UI/UX (React):**
    *   V `QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx` pridám stav `editingId`.
    *   Pri kliknutí na ikonu ceruzky na karte existujúcej akcie sa editor (horná karta) automaticky predvyplní dátami danej akcie.
    *   Tlačidlo "Vytvoriť akciu" sa dynamicky zmení na "Aktualizovať akciu" a pribudne možnosť "Zrušiť úpravu".
3.  **Vizuál:**
    *   Použijeme existujúci industrial štýl. Do zoznamu akcií pridáme modrú ikonu `Edit`. Pri aktívnej úprave sa karta editora jemne zvýrazní animáciou.

entries.description: Pridanie funkcie onUpdateQuickAction pre modifikáciu existujúcich rýchlych akcií v rámci dokumentu settings/quick_actions.
Implementácia režimu úprav v QA Architect. Pridané sledovanie editingId, reset formulára a vizuálna signalizácia režimu úprav.
